items.title: The Bayeux tapestry arrives in London for display at British Museum
items.description: The thousand-year-old, 70-metre-long Bayeux Tapestry, which depicts William the Conqueror's campaign in medieval England, has been carefully removed from its transport crate following its transfer from France. Conservators will begin a detailed condition assessment on Monday before the tapestry is installed in a specially designed display case later in the week.

items.title: India bans march on parliament by ‘Cockroach’ activists, risking a standoff
items.description: Police in New Delhi on Sunday banned a planned march on the Indian parliament by supporters of the viral, opposition Cockroach Janata Party. The demonstration set for Monday, the start of parliament's monsoonsession, follows growing calls for the education minister's resignation over exam paper leaks and the forced removal of a hunger-striking activist to hospital.

items.title: Algeria seeks larger EU energy role amid regional tensions

items.title: Kuwait, Bahrain respond to Iranian attacks amid growing escalation
items.description: The risk of a wider war in the Middle East is growing after authorities in Bahrain and Kuwait said they intercepted missiles and drones launched from Iran. Air raid sirens also sounded in Jordan's capital, Amman, while Israel said it detected Iranian missiles targeting the coastal city of Aqaba. The escalation comes after the US Embassy in Jordan urged American citizens, in a social media post, to avoid Aqaba's airport and seaport because of a specific and credible threat. Teaching Fellow in US politics and international security at University of Portsmouth, Dafydd Townley, shares his insights.

items.title: Europe: Extreme weather fuels climate concerns
items.description: Europe is the world's fastest-warming continent, and this summer has already brought record-breaking temperatures, devastating wildfires, hailstorms and tornadoes. While climate change is not responsible for every extreme weather event, scientists say it is making such events more frequent and more intense. As the impacts continue to grow, the outlook for the future is increasingly concerning. Julitte Brown reports.

items.title: Cuban dissident artist arrives in US after 5-years in prison
items.description: A hero's welcome for Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara as he arrived in Miami following his release from prison in Cuba. The dissident artist accepted exile after the United States granted him humanitarian parole. Otero Alcántara co-founded the San Isidro Movement, a collective of Havana-based artists, writers, and musicians that protests against the Cuban government. In 2021, he was arrested in Havana during anti-government demonstrations and was later sentenced to five years in prison on charges of public disorder, contempt, and disrespect for national symbols. Carla Colomé Santiago, a Cuban journalist based in Miami, shares her insights.

items.title: Russia hits Kyiv with ballistic missiles
items.description: Russia has launched one of its largest ballistic missile attacks of the conflict in Ukraine. At least one person was killed and 16 others were injured after around two dozen ballistic missiles struck Kyiv overnight. Witnesses described charred apartment buildings with windows blown out, mangled debris, and overturned cars scattered across the city. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said six districts of the capital were hit. France 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports from Kyiv.

items.title: Eight-month expedition to study the impact of climate change
items.description: As Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, an adaptation of Homer's epic poem, arrives in cinemas, a very different odyssey is about to begin. Next month, a team of scientists will travel to the remote Arctic town of Kirkenes in Norway to board the floating laboratory Tara Polar Station for an eight-month expedition. The mission will study the impact of climate change and pollution on the Arctic Ocean. For more, we're joined by Marcel Babin, Scientific Director of the Tara Polaris I expedition.

items.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister
items.description: Andy Burnham has been confirmed as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The former Manchester mayor has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

items.title: "Shangwe, le bal": a dance project full of joy
items.description: On a sweltering afternoon in the French city of Lyon, around 60 dancers fully ignored the heat to immerse themselves in a festive performance brought to them by Kenyan-American choreographer and dancer Wanjiru Kamuyu.

items.title: England stun France in goal fest for third place
items.description: England finish third after dominating in the first half. France managed to claw its way back into the game, but did not manage to find the win for Didier Deschamps' last match as French manager. Mbappé becomes the highest scoring player in world cup history, and Michael Olise matches Lionel Messi's all-time assist record. Meanwhile, Spain are hoping to get another star on their jersey, whilst Argentina is looking to achieve back-to-back world cup victories.

items.title: U.S., Iran have blown past red lines as they lurch back toward all-out war
items.description: A return to full-scale war between U.S. and Iran that would further destabilise West Asia and disrupt the world economy appears increasingly likely

items.title: Control, threats, disfiguring surgery: My life inside Jeffrey Epstein's 'cult'
items.description: "Anya" gives the BBC a rare account of how sex-criminal financier Epstein lured and abused his “assistants”.

items.title: Five candidates confirmed for Sao Tome and Principe presidential race
items.description: The Constitutional Court validated, on 16 June, five of the six candidacies: the incumbent President Carlos Vila Nova, running for a second term; former Prime Minister Jorge Bom Jesus; the parliamentary leader of the ADI, Nito D’Abreu; as well as jurists Miques João Bonfim and Eugénio Tiny.

items.title: Social media influencers Andrew, Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
items.description: Andrew Tate has amassed over 10 million followers on X but has been banned from platforms like YouTube, TikTok and Instagram for violating hate speech guidelines

items.title: Bodycam footage shows airlift of girl stranded by Texas floods
items.description: Footage shows a rescuer reaching a family of three stranded by floods that destroyed their house in Uvalde county.

items.title: China pitches AI governance model for Global South
items.description: Broader context is China’s push to both develop its alternatives to Western AI models and to offer them to developing countries; President Xi Jinping announced that Beijing would provide “5,000 opportunities in AI training and seminar programmes” in the next five years

items.title: ‘Medai’ | A common platform
items.description: Political parties and groups representing Sri Lanka’s Tamil-speaking ethnic minority communities seek to voice their shared concerns

items.title: Germany massively expands federal police powers
items.description: The German government has increased the powers of its federal police, especially in the use of AI, surveillance and drone defenses. But experts warn of constitutional challenges.

items.title: From hotel bedsheet to World Cup pitch: the story of Argentina’s Malvinas flag
items.description: Daubed with ink on a hotel bedsheet, the Malvinas banner became one of the symbols of Argentina's dramatic World Cup win over England. Its creator, who spoke exclusively to Perfil and asked to remain anonymous, explained how the flag came about, why the squad won't give it back and why he'll be at Sunday's final. Leer más

items.title: Algerian PM among the mourners as victims of orphanage fire laid to rest
items.description: Eleven people killed in a fire at an Algerian orphanage were laid to rest on Thursday at a ceremony in Bakari, on the outskirts of Algiers.

items.title: Irrepressible Argentina produce the goods in epic win over England
items.description: While the Albiceleste have celebrated epic knockout wins plenty of times in the recent past, few would have tasted sweeter than this most recent success. Leer más

items.title: Will gold prices extend their record-breaking run?
items.description: Predictions suggest the price of the precious metal will keep hitting new record highs as investors seek a safe bet in turbulent times. Some forecasts say prices could double within five years.

items.title: For the Nigerian Bar Association, a time for a bold retool, By Chidi Anselm Odinkalu
items.description: Howsoever it ends, the association must confront with bold transparency the duty to continuously improve the administration of its leadership processes. It must also understand that failure to do so will endanger its commitment to promoting the rule of law. This is not an option as the country confronts a bumpy election year in 2027. items.title: German tenants need better heat protection
items.description: Heat waves are becoming longer, hotter and more frequent. Record temperatures have left many tenants struggling. Now Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said the building law needs to be amended.

items.description: Argentina captain added another chapter to his astonishing career on Wednesday, inspiring his team to victory in their semi-final against England. Leer más

items.description: Otero Alcantara, designated a prisoner of conscience by Amnesty International, was sentenced to five years in prison in 2022 for insulting national symbols, contempt and disturbing the public order

items.title: Latin America's unlikely football unity: cheering against Argentina
items.description: While Latin Americans have traditionally rallied behind football teams from the region who advance deep into the World Cup tournament, a flood of memes, jokes and criticism has made it clear there is one exception: Argentina. Leer más

items.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Friday said Brazil would not accept any other country "disrespecting" the nation as he commented on a the trade dispute with the United States

items.title: Live: Joyce says One Nation does not support White Australia policy
items.description: Barnaby Joyce says his party does not support the White Australia policy after Pauline Hanson blamed the end of the policy for Australia's migration issues. Follow live.

items.description: The indictment stated that the two individuals had "filmed the scene of the killing and transmitted the footage to television channels" abroad.

items.description: Climate advocate Mina Hasman’s vision of designing cities that behave like forests and buildings that act like trees.

items.description: More than 50 defendants have been on trial in Italy over the 2018 bridge collapse which killed 43 people.

items.title: U.S. lawmaker urges Congress to review arms sales to India
items.description: Congressman Jim McGovern, co-chair of the Tom Lantos Human Rights Commission, also called on Congress to pass the bipartisan and bicameral Transnational Repression Policy Act

items.title: Classic winter week ahead, dangerous surf to ease in south-east
items.description: The Bureau of Meteorology says showers from the Wide Bay to the Gold Coast are possible today, with hazardous surf conditions forecast to weaken.

items.description: Washington ‌and Tehran have ​exchanged strikes after a ceasefire ⁠agreement fell apart last week, ‌raising fears of a return to all-out war

items.title: Ukraine: Thousands take to the streets to protest against Fedorov removal
items.description: Protests continued across Ukraine for a third consecutive day despite the ongoing war, with demonstrators demanding the reinstatement of the popular former defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, following his removal. The demonstrations coincided with two days of meetings between President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and the country's top military commanders.

items.title: US strikes Iran after 2 service members killed in Jordan
items.description: The US military struck Iran after two US soldiers were reported killed in an Iranian attack on a US base in Jordan. Meanwhile, Kuwait and Bahrain reported fresh Iranian attacks. DW has the latest.

items.title: World Cup Final: La Roja, La Albiceleste battle for glory
items.description: La Roja's patient possession game meets the Albiceleste's trademark resilience in the World Cup final in New York on Sunday. As Spain coach Luis de la Fuente and his former coaching student Lionel Scaloni, now Argentina's manager, prepare to face off on football's biggest stage, the showdown promises a clash between the tournament's most polished side and the defending champions who have made a habit of surviving against the odds. France 24 jounalist Selina Sykes reports the latest from New York and France 24 International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani shares her insights.

items.title: Nuits d'Afrique festival returns for 40th time
items.description: Forty years ago, Lamine Touré, a native of Côte d’Ivoire and owner of the legendary Balattou club, and Suzanne Rousseau launched this ambitious project to celebrate African cultures

items.title: 20 children killed in bus crash in Uganda
items.description: Twenty children and one adult were killed on Friday when their bus overturned in Uganda.

items.title: US slaps 25% tariffs on Brazil with election looming
items.description: The US will impose a 25% tariff on most imports from Brazil starting later this month. It's the first action in the Trump administration's new tariff strategy, after the Supreme Court struck down its prior impositions.
